_meta: BOHEMIA faction relationship graph. Derived from GDD v2 §9. Power = ordinal rank (1=weakest) per act. relations are directional labels the engine reads for spillover, war state, and AI. All canon; nothing invented.

Reds.note: Economic engine, compound-interest faction. Not military.

Blues.note: Environmentalist. Large headcount. Collective decision-making = strength and tactical liability.

Anarchists.note: Absorbed Rockstar faction. Culturally huge, territorially inconsistent. Overwhelming when provoked.

Colorful.note: LGBTQ faction. Smallest selectable. Community-based not territorial. Members across friendly factions.

Church.note: Best crisis organization. More powerful act1 than act3, ceiling drops as world heals.

Network.note: The Amalgamation's manufactured protection. Only faction with a manufactured origin. Most dangerous by act3. Strongest national/international ties.

Trades.note: Skilled-worker guild. Never takes a public position. Best for vehicle/solar/construction. Most capable civilian drones.

Caravans.note: Neutral outside-world trade. Everyone but Cartel wants them alive. Neutrality is survival, not philosophy.

Volunteers.note: Healthcare/education/food/knowledge. Resource-poor by design. Nobody wants to be seen attacking them, even Cartel stays hands-off.

Remnants.note: Military/law-enforcement survivors incl Nellis. The floor of civilization. Refuses to be a government. Biggest act1 head start. Military drones.

Cartel.note: Organized human predation. Supply chains mirror Caravan routes. Only faction the game frames as evil.

Mob.note: Controls the Strip. Ownership/protection/tribute. Historic early-police DNA, honor code = stability.

Homeless.note: Sewer/tunnel HQ under the King Hobo. Most Amalgamation-resistant (barely use feed, talk face-to-face in tunnels). Live directly above the Network's secret servers without knowing. KEY act3 liberation ally.

Custom.note: Player faction. No preset philosophy. Identity emerges from three generations of action.

Pures.note: white supremacist group. Members inside other factions. Larger in act1 (crash drove identity clustering), fixed ceiling, stagnant across acts.

Panthers.note: black supremacist group. Members inside other factions. Larger in act1 (crash drove identity clustering), fixed ceiling, stagnant across acts.

La Familia.note: Latino supremacist group. Members inside other factions. Larger in act1 (crash drove identity clustering), fixed ceiling, stagnant across acts.

Triads.note: Asian supremacist group. Members inside other factions. Larger in act1 (crash drove identity clustering), fixed ceiling, stagnant across acts.
